description: A circular object that revolves on an axis; specifically the spinning wheel or potter's wheel. 'Rolu' names what turns in cycles—rotation enabling transformation.
sound_symbolism: The word rotates round. 'Ro' rolls in continuous motion, wheel turning. Then 'lu' flows onward, cycle unending.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo rolu' (the wheel). Primarily craft wheels (spinning, pottery) rather than transport. Related to cyclical motion.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The wheel as transformation—spinning fiber to thread, clay to vessel, through patient rotation.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Cycles of change—the wheel turns, nothing remains fixed, transformation is constant.
